item.title: The Most Frightening Shooters Are the Smart Ones
item.description: A manifesto-like email allegedly sent by the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner shooter suggests a murderous obsession with Trump’s politics.
item.dc:creator: Graeme Wood

item.title: We Cannot Harden the World Against Every Attacker
item.description: Tragedy was averted at a Washington hotel, but such moments will happen again.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nichols

item.title: A Dark New Litmus Test for Power in Washington
item.description: The shooting at the correspondents’ dinner made clear who gets saved first.
item.dc:creator: Michael Scherer

item.title: MAGA’s Strange Quiet After the Shooting
item.description: Instead of a crackdown on his enemies, Trump wants his ballroom.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: Two Poems by Luis Muñoz
item.dc:creator: Luis Muñoz

item.title: Thou Shalt Not Post the Ten Commandments in Classrooms
item.description: Requiring schools to endorse biblical laws is both unconstitutional and counterproductive.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Bruenig

item.title: How Netanyahu Hurt America’s Jews
item.description: The Israeli prime minister’s focus is, as always, on himself and his near-term political needs. The plight of American Jews is simply not his concern.
item.dc:creator: Michael A. Cohen

item.title: A Shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: The president is safe after chaos at the Washington Hilton, and a suspect is reportedly in custody.
item.dc:creator: Missy Ryan

item.title: How Trump Voters Are Reacting to the Economy
item.description: Panelists joined to discuss the president’s recent polling numbers, and more.
item.dc:creator: The Editors

item.title: The U.K. Smoking Ban Is Illiberal
item.description: Distaste for cigarettes is no reason to cede bedrock liberties to the state.
item.dc:creator: Conor Friedersdorf

item.title: The Strange Comfort of a Rewatch
item.description: Even in the age of infinite options, people keep returning to the movies, shows, songs, and books they know by heart.
item.dc:creator: Rafaela Jinich

item.title: The Republican Who Outsmarted Trump
item.description: Thomas Massie is one of the few Republicans who is unafraid of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Russell Berman

item.title: Where Is Tulsi Gabbard?
item.description: Her irrelevance during a war suggests that America doesn’t need a director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nichols

item.title: Why Silicon Valley Is Turning to the Catholic Church
item.description: Priests and theologians want to shape the future of AI. Big Tech is listening.
item.dc:creator: Elias Wachtel

item.title: Sam Altman Wants to Know Whether You’re Human
item.description: And he has a way to prove it.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: How Short-Form Clips Took Over the Internet
item.description: You’re not watching the show. You’re watching the clips.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Warzel

item.title: Who Came Up With That?
item.description: Contrary to what we think of as intellectual property, most ideas are difficult to trace back to one human mind.
item.dc:creator: Boris Kachka

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: New Deal, Old Dynasty
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Photos of the Week: Fallen Robot, Scooter Waterfall, Sunrise Panorama
item.description: Traditional horseback archery in Japan, a humanoid-robot half-marathon in Beijing, a sci-fi festival in England, a golf tournament in Louisiana, and much more
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Texans Will Decide if Jesus Was a Lefty
item.description: James Talarico is trying to sell a novel brand of Christian politics in a deep-red state.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Bruenig
